articles.title: Heatwave Grips Maharashtra; Brahmapuri Scorches at 47.1°C Amid Red Alert in Vidarbha - lokmattimes.com
articles.full_text:
Heatwave Grips Maharashtra; Brahmapuri Scorches at 47.1°C Amid Red Alert in Vidarbha
By Lokmat Times Desk | Updated: May 24, 2026 07:52 IST2026-05-24T07:52:20+5:302026-05-24T07:52:52+5:30
Blistering heat and scorching summer winds swept across several parts of India on Saturday, pushing temperatures past 44 degrees ...
Blistering heat and scorching summer winds swept across several parts of India on Saturday, pushing temperatures past 44 degrees Celsius in multiple regions.
In Maharashtra, Brahmapuri recorded the highest maximum temperature at 47.1 degrees Celsius, followed by Chandrapur and Gadchiroli at 46 degrees Celsius.
The Regional Meteorological Centre of the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a warning for severe heatwave conditions in Vidarbha over the next four days. According to the forecast issued for May 24 to 27, temperatures are expected to remain significantly above normal in several districts.
Heatwave conditions are likely to prevail in Amravati, Wardha, Chandrapur and Yavatmal districts, prompting authorities to issue red alerts in some areas.
The intense weather conditions have also affected other parts of the country. In Punjab, hospitals have operationalised heat stroke management units to deal with rising cases linked to extreme temperatures, while the IMD has issued a heatwave alert for three districts in Jharkhand for Monday.
The soaring temperatures have begun impacting animals and birds as well. Veterinarians and animal rescuers in Delhi reported cases of pigeons collapsing unconscious from the sky, dehydrated eagles being rescued from roadsides, and stray animals suffering from stomach toxicity amid the capital’s relentless heatwave.
